medications.name: Prednisone
medications.prescriber: Dr. Sarah Beckman / Dr. Jessica Tanaka
medications.indication: Post-pulse maintenance for active SLE with inflammatory myositis
medications.instructions: Take in AM with food. PLANNED TAPER: 30 mg current week → 25 mg next week → decrease by 5 mg every 2 weeks to 10 mg → then by 2.5 mg monthly. Do NOT stop abruptly. Stress-dose education given. Call clinic for any signs of infection.
medications.pharmacy: Hopkins Outpatient Pharmacy / Walgreens Patterson Park
medications.notes: Initiated post-pulse-MP (1g IV daily x 3 days on HD8-10, 3/22-3/24/2027). Insurance: emergency Medicaid -> permanent Medicaid approved 4/8/2027.
medications.history: Patient previously received intermittent prednisone bursts at lower doses (10 mg taper 4/2026; 30 mg from 11/4/2026 onward at varying doses).

medications.name: Mycophenolate mofetil
medications.indication: Steroid-sparing immunosuppression for active SLE with inflammatory myositis
medications.instructions: Take with food to reduce GI side effects. Contraception REQUIRED — pregnancy must be avoided. PCP prophylaxis with TMP/SMX required. Monthly labs (CBC, CMP, MMF levels) for first 3 months.
medications.notes: Restarted during AP3 admission on HD5 (3/19/2027) at 500 mg BID, advanced to 1 g BID on 3/21, to 1.5 g BID on 4/8/2027. Maintenance plan 12-18 months minimum.
medications.history: Originally prescribed 11/9/2026 at 500 mg BID -> 1 g BID by 11/30/2026. Stopped ~3/9/2027 (supply exhausted before admission). Restarted in hospital.

medications.name: Hydroxychloroquine
medications.prescriber: Dr. Manuel Reyes (initial); continued by Dr. Tanaka
medications.instructions: Take with food. Annual ophthalmology screening required (baseline completed 4/3/2027 - normal).
medications.notes: Continuous since admission to hospital 3/15/2027. Patient has had multiple prior interruptions related to PAP / access barriers — now covered by Medicaid (approved 4/8/2027).

medications.name: Levothyroxine
medications.prescriber: Dr. Manuel Reyes (initial); continued by Esperanza PCP and rheumatology
medications.indication: Hashimoto's thyroiditis (TSH 5.32 initial; TPO Ab positive)
medications.instructions: Take 30-60 minutes before breakfast with water. Avoid taking with food, calcium, iron supplements, or PPIs within 4 hours.
medications.notes: Dose increased from 25 mcg to 50 mcg during admission 3/16/2027 due to TSH 6.84 and free T4 0.6 with overlying sick euthyroid pattern. TSH 2.84 on 4/22/2027.

medications.name: Trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole
medications.instructions: Continue until off prednisone for >3 months. Notify clinic for rash, fever, mucosal symptoms (possible AHS).
medications.notes: Initiated at AMA discharge 2/11/2027. Confirmed continued during 3/15/2027 admission.

medications.name: Calcium carbonate
medications.indication: Bone protection on corticosteroids; baseline hypocalcemia
medications.instructions: Take with food. Separate from levothyroxine and iron by ≥4 hours.

medications.name: Vitamin D3 (cholecalciferol)
medications.notes: Increased from 1000 IU at discharge given baseline deficiency.

medications.name: Omeprazole
medications.instructions: Take 30 minutes before breakfast. Separate from levothyroxine by ≥4 hours.

medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.instructions: Max 3 g/day. Avoid alcohol. Counseled re: LFT monitoring.
medications.notes: Ibuprofen discontinued given lupus nephritis history; APAP is the preferred analgesic.

medications.name: Multivitamin
medications.indication: General nutritional support during recovery from severe malnutrition

_historical_notes: Hydroxychloroquine had several gaps (8/2026 to 9/2026 and 10/2026 to 11/2026) related to insurance/PAP barriers — now resolved with Medicaid coverage. Mycophenolate restarted at higher dose during admission. Prednisone post-pulse maintenance. Zinc, selenium, and thiamine prescribed during admission and discontinued after completing repletion courses by mid-April 2027.
